source_jobs.title: Client Solutions Associate, San Francisco - Immediate Start
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Office Hours is an on-demand expert network that connects leading organizations with trusted experts across various knowledge domains. Experts earn income by sharing their knowledge through advisory work, projects, and AI model training. Our platform handles the complexities behind the scenes— screening, compliance, scheduling, and payments—so knowledge sharing stays focused on meaningful insights and real impact.

We’re a hyper-growth and profitable company, quickly expanding our expert network, launching new offices, and new products. We are headquartered in San Francisco, with offices in Brooklyn and Bangalore. Our customers include the fastest-growing digital health companies, technology companies, institutional investment firms, consulting firms and AI Labs. We are backed by top marketplace investors and operators of companies like DoorDash, Airbnb, Affirm.




WHAT WE BELIEVE

Human knowledge is the world’s most valuable asset. And yet, despite being more interconnected than ever, most knowledge still remains stuck in our heads, inaccessible and underutilized. Our vision is to make human knowledge easily accessible and infinitely scalable by building tools for the new age knowledge economy.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Our Client Solutions team is the front line for finding the right experts for our clients. As a Client Solutions Associate, you will be directly responsible for growing our network and scaling our impact by connecting our clients to the right subject matter experts, who will then share their knowledge through qualitative interviews.

This role is fast-paced and no day is the same. You will need to be hard-working, flexible, with strong client-facing communication skills. You will be part of creating and improving processes to help us do better work every day. You will work alongside our product and engineering team to translate client feedback into product improvements.

We are looking for candidates that are excited to face the successes and challenges of growing a business and developing a loyal base of users.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Though no day is the same as a CSA, you can expect the job to involve:

 - Client-Expert Connections: You'll identify and match experts to clients for interview-based research needs, continuously communicating with clients and experts to track the progress of these connections to ensure high-quality outcomes and client satisfaction.

 - Project Creation & Ownership: Manage your clients’ research projects, from understanding their goals to recruiting and scheduling the right experts for them. Recruiting experts involves using our internal network and external channels like social media, meticulously reviewing professional profiles, reaching out to potential experts, and strategically matching them to specific project requirements.

 - Project Performance Tracking: Monitor client and expert feedback on interviews to 1) drive successful research for our clients and 2) ensure our network has only high-quality, engaged experts.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Share insights from client and expert interactions to help product and engineering teams continuously refine the Office Hours online platform. Your frontline experience will provide critical input for user experience improvements.

This role demands strategic thinking, strong communication skills, and a proactive approach to solving projects.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - You are not afraid of ambitious goals

 - You are a great communicator

 - You are an optimist with a growth mindset

 - You thrive when you truly own something

 - You are an independent problem-solver

 - 0-2 years of professional work experience


BONUS EXPERIENCE

 - Prior experience working at a startup

 - Prior experience in a customer success role

 - Strong understanding of various professional industries and roles


BENEFITS + PERKS

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k)

 - Monthly wellness and fitness sti

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Office Hours is an on-demand consulting platform that connects leading organizations with trusted experts across various domains. Experts earn income by sharing their knowledge through advisory work, projects, and AI model training. Our platform handles the complexities behind the scenes, screening, compliance, scheduling, and payments, so knowledge sharing stays focused on meaningful insights and real impact.

We’re a hyper-growth, profitable company, quickly expanding our expert network, launching new offices, and building new products. We are headquartered in San Francisco, with offices in Brooklyn and Bangalore. Our customers include the fastest-growing digital health companies, technology companies, institutional investment firms, consulting firms, and AI Labs. We are backed by top marketplace investors and operators of companies like DoorDash, Airbnb, and Affirm.




WHAT WE BELIEVE

Human knowledge is the world’s most valuable asset. And yet, despite being more interconnected than ever, most knowledge still remains stuck in our heads, inaccessible and underutilized. Our vision is to make human knowledge easily accessible and infinitely scalable by building tools for the new age knowledge economy.




HOW WE THINK ABOUT DESIGN

Design is foundational to Office Hours.

We believe quality comes from judgment, care, and attention to detail, especially when building complex systems. Speed matters, but not at the expense of coherence or craft. We aim to build products that feel clear, durable, and considered, not rushed or over-optimized.

We care deeply about visual design, typography, consistency, and restraint. We trust intuition alongside data, and we regularly ask whether something feels right, not just whether it ships or converts. We’d rather ship a smaller surface that feels finished than a larger one that doesn’t.

This role reports to the Head of Product, who has designed the platform end-to-end since the beginning and remains closely involved in product design.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Senior Product Designer with strong taste and experience shipping high-quality products in complex environments.

This role is suited for someone with 5+ years of experience who can own meaningful product areas end-to-end, from shaping problems to refining execution. You’ll work closely with product and engineering partners to design thoughtful, scalable experiences across a two-sided marketplace where trust, clarity, and quality matter.

You’ll be expected to set a high bar for craft, help guide decisions through design judgment, and raise the overall quality of the product through your work and influence.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own and design end-to-end experiences across expert, client, and internal product surfaces.

 - Partner with product and engineering to define problems, explore solutions, and ship features thoughtfully.

 - Translate complex requirements into clear user flows, interactions, and visual systems.

 - Craft high-quality UI with strong attention to typography, spacing, hierarchy, and brand.

 - Prototype and iterate quickly, then refine designs to a consistently high polish.

 - Contribute to and evolve shared patterns, systems, and design foundations.

 - Help raise the design bar through critique, feedback, and example.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - 5+ years of experience in product design, UX/UI design, or a related role.

 - A portfolio that demonstrates strong visual design, typography, and taste across shipped products.

 - Experience designing complex workflows, systems, or multi-surface products.

 - Strong fundamentals in interaction design, usability, and information hierarchy.

 - Comfort working closely with engineers and product partners from concept through launch.

 - Clear communication skills and a principled approach to design decisions.

 - A consistent track record of care, judgment, and quality in your work.

Strong command of visuals, typography, and brand sensibility is s

source_jobs.title: Client Solutions Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Office Hours is an on-demand expert network that connects leading organizations with trusted experts across various knowledge domains. Experts earn income by sharing their knowledge through advisory work, projects, and AI model training. Our platform handles the complexities behind the scenes— screening, compliance, scheduling, and payments—so knowledge sharing stays focused on meaningful insights and real impact.

We’re a hyper-growth and profitable company, quickly expanding our expert network, launching new offices, and new products. We are headquartered in San Francisco, with offices in Brooklyn and Bangalore. Our customers include the fastest-growing digital health companies, technology companies, institutional investment firms, consulting firms and AI Labs. We are backed by top marketplace investors and operators of companies like DoorDash, Airbnb, Affirm.




WHAT WE BELIEVE

Human knowledge is the world’s most valuable asset. And yet, despite being more interconnected than ever, most knowledge still remains stuck in our heads, inaccessible and underutilized. Our vision is to make human knowledge easily accessible and infinitely scalable by building tools for the new age knowledge economy.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Client Solutions Manager, you will own a portfolio of enterprise customers and will be directly responsible for their growth. You will design and executive your own account acquisition and account expansion strategies while coaching a high-performance team. Your book of business will consist of the most prominent investment firms in the region.

This role is fast-paced and client-facing with a focus on client portfolio growth and team development. You will spend most of your time developing growth strategies, executing projects, and coaching top performers. As a Client Solutions leader and first point of contact for our clients, you will also work alongside our product and engineering team to translate insights into product improvements.

Ideal candidates have prior experience managing client-facing teams within enterprise business development and/or customer success. They love taking on new challenges while never taking things for granted. We are looking for candidates who are excited to face the successes and challenges of growing client portfolios and developing high-performance teams.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Grow a portfolio of enterprise customers through account acquisition and account expansion strategies

 - Hire and coach top performers and maintain talent density

 - Innovate and improve processes and policies within the Client Solutions department


WHAT YOU BRING

 - You display a “challenge-accepted” mentality

 - You are passionate about training, coaching, and developing talent

 - You inspire and motivate your team to achieve ambitious goals

 - You are results-driven and excel at identifying new & innovative ways to drive growth

 - 3-5 years of professional work experience in customer-facing roles; 1-2 years of experience managing a client-facing team


BENEFITS + PERKS

 - Competitive Salary & Stock Options

 - Healthcare, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401(k)

 - Wellness/fitness benefit

 - Flexible vacation time, regular company holidays

 - Company retreats (Tahoe, Maui, Mendocino, Mexico City)

 - Parent-friendly, remote work, and paid family leave

Join our team and help everyone realize the true power of what they know!

Pay Transparency Notice:

Full-time offers include base salary, equity, and benefits

Pay range: $110,000 – $130,000, based on seniority and relevant experience

This is a hybrid role, in our NYC office 2-3 times per week.

Don’t meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that some candidates, especially underrepresented groups such as women and people of color, are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At Office Hours we believe in building a diverse and inclusive workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but

source_jobs.title: Client Solutions Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Office Hours is an on-demand expert network that connects leading organizations with trusted experts across various knowledge domains. Experts earn income by sharing their knowledge through advisory work, projects, and AI model training. Our platform handles the complexities behind the scenes screening, compliance, scheduling, and payment so knowledge sharing stays focused on meaningful insights and real impact.

We’re a hyper-growth and profitable company, quickly expanding our expert network, launching new offices, and new products. We are headquartered in San Francisco, with offices in Brooklyn and Bangalore. Our customers include the fastest-growing digital health companies, technology companies, institutional investment firms, consulting firms and AI Labs. We are backed by top marketplace investors and operators of companies like DoorDash, Airbnb, and Affirm.




WHAT WE BELIEVE

Human knowledge is the world’s most valuable asset. And yet, despite being more interconnected than ever, most knowledge still remains stuck in our heads, inaccessible and underutilized. Our vision is to make human knowledge easily accessible and infinitely scalable by building tools for the new age knowledge economy.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Director, Client Solutions to lead and scale our Client Solutions organization. This role is responsible for delivering exceptional client outcomes while driving sustainable revenue growth across our customer base.

The Director will own segment performance while leading a team of Managers, Client Success Managers (CSMs), and Client Solutions Associates (CSAs). You will ensure our clients receive exceptional service, projects run smoothly, and accounts expand over time through strong delivery and trusted relationships.

This role requires a leader who can balance commercial performance, operational excellence, and team development. You will help scale the Client Solutions organization while maintaining the high standards and culture that define Office Hours.

This role reports directly to the Head of Client Solutions and works closely with Sales, Product, and Leadership.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


OWN SEGMENT PERFORMANCE

Drive revenue growth, account expansion, and customer retention across the Client Solutions organization. Ensure the team consistently meets or exceeds revenue goals while maintaining strong client relationships.


BUILD AND DEVELOP THE TEAM

Lead hiring, coaching, and professional development for Managers, CSMs, and CSAs. Maintain high talent density by developing strong performers and making thoughtful hiring decisions.


LEAD CLIENT DELIVERY EXCELLENCE

Ensure projects run smoothly across accounts, customer relationships remain strong, and delivery quality remains consistently high.


LEAD THE CLIENT SOLUTIONS ORGANIZATION

Continuously improve how the Client Solutions organization operates and scales. This includes shaping onboarding programs, strengthening professional development and training, improving operational playbooks, and raising the performance bar across the team.

You will also bring insights from client work back into the company to improve our product, platform, and customer experience.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Experience leading Client Success, Account Management, or Customer Solutions teams in a high-growth environment

 - Strong track record of driving revenue growth, account expansion, and customer retention

 - Proven ability to hire, coach, and develop high-performing teams

 - Operational mindset with the ability to improve systems, processes, and execution at scale

 - Ability to balance strategic thinking with hands-on leadership

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate across Sales, Product, and Leadership

 - High standards for performance and strong accountability for results


BONUS EXPERIENCE

 - Experience leading client-facing teams in marketplace, consulting, or expert network businesses

 - Background working with e

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Grow a portfolio of enterprise customers through account acquisition and account expansion strategies

 - Set the standard for expert experience - ensuring experts receive a high-quality experience and maintaining a high bar for trust, respect, and value on the supply side of our marketplace.

 - Innovate and improve processes and policies within the Client Solutions department

source_jobs.title: Account Executive, Expert Data Services
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Office Hours is an on-demand expert network that connects leading organizations with trusted experts across various knowledge domains. Experts earn income by sharing their knowledge through advisory work, projects, and AI model training. Our platform handles the complexities behind the scenes— screening, compliance, scheduling, and payments—so knowledge sharing stays focused on meaningful insights and real impact.

We’re a hyper-growth and profitable company, quickly expanding our expert network, launching new offices, and new products. We are headquartered in San Francisco, with offices in Brooklyn and Bangalore. Our customers include the fastest-growing digital health companies, technology companies, institutional investment firms, consulting firms and AI Labs. We are backed by top marketplace investors and operators of companies like DoorDash, Airbnb, Affirm.


WHAT WE BELIEVE

Human knowledge is the world’s most valuable asset. And yet, despite being more interconnected than ever, most knowledge still remains stuck in our heads, inaccessible and underutilized. Our vision is to make human knowledge easily accessible and infinitely scalable by building tools for the new age knowledge economy.


ABOUT THE ROLE

The Account Executive owns new revenue for Office Hours' Expert Data business. The EDS team is working at the frontier of AI capabilities across medicine and biology, pairing US board-certified physicians and practicing scientists with frontier AI labs to power model training and evaluation.
You will sell to research, data, and post-training teams at leading AI labs and other companies building frontier AI systems. These are technical buyers who care about data quality and domain rigor, so you will learn what each lab is building, show how our expert network moves their models forward, and convert that into large, multi-year commitments.
Success means winning new logos, growing Total Contract Value (TCV), expanding accounts through upsell and cross-sell, and building lasting relationships with senior decision makers.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

Hit and exceed quarterly TCV targets
Consistently close new business that meets or exceeds the quarterly Total Contract Value (TCV) quota, generating predictable revenue growth for the company. Own your number end to end and forecast it accurately.

You’ll do this by:

 1. Win new customer logos
    Acquire new logos by identifying qualified opportunities, executing strong sales processes, and converting trials or pilots into long-term contracts. Land the first deal in a way that sets up expansion down the line.

 2. Drive upsell and expansion into existing accounts
    Identify each key customer's priorities by reading their published benchmarks, papers, and research, then reach out with the specific topical areas they're focused on, land meetings with the right teams, and sell new work that maps to their roadmap. Turn a single engagement into a growing, multi-workstream relationship.

 3. Build trusted relationships with senior buyers
    Develop durable relationships with research, data, and post-training leaders at frontier labs and healthcare and pharma corporates. Represent Office Hours as a category leader in expert data for health and life sciences.


WHAT YOU BRING

 - 3+ years of enterprise sales experience with a consistent record of meeting and exceeding quota

 - A proven ability to close large, multi-year contracts and grow contract value over time with upselling and expanding to new teams

 - Comfort running complex, multi-stakeholder deals with long sales cycles and multiple decision makers

 - A track record of generating your own pipeline through outbound, referrals, and network, not just closing inbound

 - Experience selling a technical solution and working with technical team-members to craft consultative solutions for customers that meet specific customer objectives

 - You are persuasive, persistent and ambitious with a consistent hunger to take on big cha

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR PLATFORM ENGINEER (SF, NYC, OR REMOTE)




ABOUT US

Office Hours is an on-demand expert network that connects leading organizations with trusted experts across various knowledge domains. Experts earn income by sharing their knowledge through advisory work, projects, and AI model training. Our platform handles the complexities behind the scenes— screening, compliance, scheduling, and payments—so knowledge sharing stays focused on meaningful insights and real impact.

We're a hyper-growth and profitable company, quickly expanding our expert network, launching new offices, and new products. We are headquartered in San Francisco, with offices in Brooklyn and Bangalore. Our customers include the fastest-growing digital health companies, technology companies, institutional investment firms, consulting firms and AI Labs. We are backed by top marketplace investors and operators of companies like DoorDash, Airbnb, Affirm.




WHAT WE BELIEVE

Human knowledge is the world's most valuable asset. And yet, despite being more interconnected than ever, most knowledge still remains stuck in our heads, inaccessible and underutilized. Our vision is to make human knowledge easily accessible and infinitely scalable by building tools for the new age knowledge economy.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Senior Platform Engineer at Office Hours, you'll own the infrastructure and platform that power our marketplace and the products our experts and customers depend on every day. Your focus will be keeping our systems reliable, observable, secure, and easy to operate—with a strong emphasis on automation, security, and operational excellence.

This is a high-ownership role on a fast-growing, high-leverage team. You'll partner closely with product and platform engineers to reduce operational toil, harden our security posture, and give teams the confidence to ship quickly and safely as we scale.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Cloud & Kubernetes: Run and scale production AWS and EKS — cluster lifecycle, autoscaling, and the platform add-ons that keep it healthy (ingress, DNS, certificates, secrets).

 - Infrastructure as Code: Manage cloud and cluster configuration declaratively with Terraform, Helm, Kustomize, eksctl and GitOps.

 - CI/CD & GitOps: Ship through GitHub Actions and GitOps (Argo CD / Flux), with automated build, test, and dependency gating.

 - Security & access: SAST/DAST scanning, secrets management, and least-privilege IAM (IRSA), plus routine dependency and vulnerability remediation.

 - Observability: Own metrics, logs, and traces across Datadog, and Sentry.

 - Data & messaging: Operate PostgreSQL, MongoDB / Atlas, OpenSearch / Elasticsearch, and Temporal.

 - Languages & tooling: Build automation and internal tooling in Python, Bash, Go, and Node.js / TypeScript; containerize with Docker.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Cloud & Kubernetes: 5+ years running AWS and Kubernetes in production (EKS preferred), including platform add-ons and AWS-native integration.

 - IaC & GitOps: Strong with Terraform and Kubernetes manifests (Helm, Kustomize), plus GitOps Actions, plus GitOps delivery (Argo CD and/or Flux).

 - Security mindset: SAST/DAST, secrets management, least-privilege IAM, and vulnerability management as routine work.

 - Observability: Metrics, logs, and traces with Datadog, Sentry, or equivalents.

 - Programming: Node.js / TypeScript and Python, plus Bash scripting on Linux.

 - Data systems: PostgreSQL and MongoDB; messaging/workflows with Temporal.

 - Version control & collaboration: Git / GitHub workflows and a documentation-first, async communication style.

 - Networking fundamentals: TCP/IP, DNS, routing, load balancing.


BONUS EXPERIENCE

 - AI / ML infrastructure (model-training pipelines, vector search)

 - Cloud cost and capacity management at scale

 - Compliance-sensitive environments (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001)




PAY TRANSPARENCY NOTICE

Full-time offers include base salary, equity, and benefits.

Pay range: $160,000-$180,000 based

notes: Mostly sales and client-facing roles, with a single engineering position.
